title: ISE Exporter Health
description: Per-dataset/collector collection status (enabled, up, fresh, last success, next run, state), why collection is constrained (Data Connect duty cycle, serialized worker queue, per-collector duration), row/coverage limits (top-K and paged-scan truncation), and when each dataset becomes usable for dashboard purposes (snapshot vs id-tail counters vs the accumulated endpoint_fleet ramp).

panels.title: Dataset Collection Status
panels.description: One row per dataset/collector: State summarizes Enabled/Up/Fresh plus whether the nominal next-run time has already passed (waiting_on_queue means the interval/backoff plan says this dataset is due, the last collection is still within its freshness window, so the serialized Data Connect worker or duty-cycle cooldown -- not a failure -- is holding it up; see the Collection Constraints row). Next Run In is the scheduler's nominal next-eligible time, not a promise: Data Connect's queue and cooldown can still push the real attempt later. A negative Next Run In with State=ok is normal jitter; sustained negative values are the waiting_on_queue signal.

targets.expr: label_replace((ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0), "state", "disabled", "dataset", ".+") or label_replace(((ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)), "state", "failing", "dataset", ".+") or label_replace((((ise_dataset_fresh{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)), "state", "stale", "dataset", ".+") or label_replace(((((ise_dataset_next_run_timestamp{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} < time()) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_fresh{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)), "state", "waiting_on_queue", "dataset", ".+") or label_replace((((ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_fresh{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) unless on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_next_run_timestamp{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} < time()), "state", "ok", "dataset", ".+")

panels.title: Unavailable
panels.description: Enabled authoritative datasets whose latest collection attempt failed. Shown as a trend so a growing count is visible before it becomes an incident.

panels.title: Stale Datasets
panels.description: Enabled datasets without a successful replacement inside twice their configured interval. Shown as a trend so a growing count is visible before it becomes an incident.

panels.title: Missing Expected View Updates
panels.description: Periodic Data Connect rollup views with no row in the bounded recent window. Quiet event-driven views remain informational and do not raise this health count. Shown as a trend so a growing count is visible before it becomes an incident.

targets.expr: (count((ise_dataconnect_view_has_recent_rows == 0) and on(view,domain) (ise_dataconnect_view_freshness_expected == 1)) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_freshness",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_freshness",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_freshness",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_freshness",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))

panels.title: Truncation
panels.description: Collector dimensional breakdowns where the configured top-K limit omits groups. Exact total metrics remain authoritative. Shown as a trend so a growing count is visible before it becomes an incident.

targets.expr: ((count(ise_dataconnect_radius_topk_truncated == 1) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_radius",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_radius",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_radius",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_radius",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((max(ise_dataconnect_radius_active_groups_truncated) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_radius_active",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_radius_active",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_radius_active",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_radius_active",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((count(ise_dataconnect_posture_topk_truncated == 1) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_posture",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_posture",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_posture",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_posture",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((count(ise_dataconnect_endpoint_topk_truncated == 1) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_endpoints",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_endpoints",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_endpoints",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_endpoints",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((count(ise_dataconnect_diagnostic_topk_truncated == 1) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_performance",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_performance",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_performance",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_performance",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((count(ise_tacacs_topk_truncated == 1) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="tacacs_activity",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="tacacs_activity",source="dataconnect"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="tacacs_activity",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="tacacs_activity",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)))) + ((max(ise_tacacs_internal_user_inventory_truncated) and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="tacacs_config",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="tacacs_config",source="rest"}) == 1))) or on() (0 * ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="tacacs_config",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="tacacs_config",source="rest"}) == 1))))

panels.title: Unavailable Dataset Details
panels.description: Every enabled unavailable or stale dataset, its collection source, bounded failure category, and operator-safe detail. A NONE row explicitly confirms that all enabled datasets are available and fresh.

targets.expr: label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(((ise_dataset_last_failure_detail_info{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} or label_replace((ise_dataset_last_failure_info{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} unless on(dataset,source) ise_dataset_last_failure_detail_info{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"}), "detail", "Failure category: $1; inspect exporter journal", "reason", "(.+)") or (label_replace(label_replace(ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"}, "reason", "not_attempted", "dataset", ".+"), "detail", "No collection attempt has completed or failed yet", "dataset", ".+") unless on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_last_failure_info{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} or ise_dataset_last_failure_detail_info{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"}))) and on(dataset,source) ((ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1))) or label_replace(label_replace(((ise_dataset_fresh{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1), "reason", "stale", "dataset", ".+"), "detail", "Last successful collection is older than two configured intervals", "dataset", ".+") or label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(label_replace(absent(count(((ise_dataset_up{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0) or on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_fresh{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 0)) and on(dataset,source) (ise_dataset_enabled{dataset=~"$dataset",source=~"$source"} == 1))), "dataset", "NONE", "", ".*"), "source", "-", "", ".*"), "reason", "healthy", "", ".*"), "detail", "All enabled datasets are available and fresh", "", ".*"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-overview", "dataset", ".*"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-access-troubleshooting", "dataset", "dataconnect_radius.*|dataconnect_nad_health"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-psn-troubleshooting", "dataset", "dataconnect_performance"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-secureclient", "dataset", "dataconnect_posture|mnt_active_posture"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-tacacs", "dataset", "tacacs_.*"), "dashboard_uid", "ise-endpoints-devices", "dataset", "dataconnect_endpoints|devices")

panels.title: Dataset Collection Attempt and Success Age
panels.description: Seconds since each enabled dataset was last attempted and last replaced successfully. Missing success series mean that dataset has never completed successfully since exporter state was reset. A third series ("next run in") is the scheduler's nominal next-eligible time minus now; negative means the plan already considers this dataset due -- see Dataset Collection Status for whether that is healthy jitter or a stuck queue.

panels.title: Data Connect Source-Event Freshness
panels.description: Recent-row presence and newest recent event inside each view's configured bounded window. Each source probe returns at most one row; global emptiness, exact counts, and full-history scans are intentionally omitted to protect MnT.

panels.title: Data Connect Worker / Queue
panels.description: One worker executes reporting domains serially. Queue depth above zero is expected during a paced cold start but should eventually drain.

panels.title: Oldest Data Connect Queue Wait
panels.description: Time the oldest reporting domain has waited behind the serialized duty-cycle gate.

panels.title: MnT Posture Worker
panels.description: Whether the independent bounded MnT detail-refresh lane is currently executing.

panels.title: Data Connect Duty-Cycle Advisory
panels.description: Configured duty cycle versus the recommended 0.1-2.0% band: -1 below (the global cooldown will throttle operational datasets far below their configured cadence -- raise dataconnect.duty_cycle_percent toward the band), 0 within (healthy), 1 above (sustained high database load -- lower it back toward the band). A sustained -1 alongside large cooldowns on the panel to the right is the actionable pattern, not a momentary blip.

panels.title: Data Connect Query Cooldown (per view, trend)
panels.description: Quiet time imposed after each view query to keep aggregate Data Connect database duty cycle within the configured ceiling, over time rather than a single instant. Sustained cooldown much greater than a dataset's configured cadence means the duty cycle is starving it -- raise dataconnect.duty_cycle_percent toward 2.0 (see the advisory panel).

panels.title: Data Connect Statements per Minute
panels.description: Fixed reporting-view labels only; arbitrary SQL is never exported.

panels.title: Per-Collector Duration (trend)
panels.description: Wall-clock duration of each collector's latest completed attempt, over time. A collector whose duration creeps toward its own configured interval (or the Data Connect query timeout) is at risk of overlapping its own next attempt or starving the serialized worker queue -- investigate the slow view before raising its interval further.

panels.title: Latest Data Connect Query Duration
panels.description: Latest completed statement latency by reporting view. This remains visible across the intentionally sparse production cadence; use the histogram metric for long-range percentile analysis.

panels.title: Latest Data Connect Rows Returned
panels.description: Client-side result size by fixed reporting view; a failed latest attempt publishes zero instead of retaining an older successful row count. Grouped result caps protect exporter memory and metric cardinality.

panels.title: Data Connect Collection Cadence
panels.description: Scheduled domain cadence and bounded event scan windows. The client applies the per-statement duty-cycle cooldown shown below; the scheduler does not apply that delay a second time.

panels.title: Missing Optional Schema Columns
panels.description: Known optional columns absent from available Data Connect reporting views. These gaps degrade only dependent metric families; mandatory gaps block their owning datasets and appear above.

panels.title: Data Connect Enforced Safety Limits
panels.description: Effective hard client limits. Production should show a duty-cycle ceiling no higher than 0.1%, a query timeout no higher than 15 seconds, 6,000 rows per statement, and 64 MiB retained bytes.

panels.title: Posture Re-Assessment (6h): Assessed vs Backlog
panels.description: Posture-applicable endpoints assessed within the last 6h versus the backlog not assessed in that window (the two stack to the eligible total). Endpoints posture on connect or periodically, so a persistent backlog is normal — watch the backlog trend and drops in the assessed line, not a 100% target. For posture health use the compliance ratio and assessed-endpoint counts on the Secure Client dashboard.

panels.title: RADIUS Dimensional Breakdown Coverage (top-K)
panels.description: Groups returned versus the exact pre-limit group count for each RADIUS dimensional breakdown (authentication, latency, volume_summary, failure_context, accounting, accounting_sessions). Returned and total converging is healthy; a widening, sustained gap means the top-K limit is actively cutting real groups from that breakdown -- see the companion truncated-flag panel and raise the relevant max_groups setting if it stays truncated.

panels.title: RADIUS Dimensional Breakdown Truncated (top-K)
panels.description: Whether the Data Connect top-K limit is currently cutting each RADIUS dimensional breakdown (1=truncated). Exact totals stay authoritative in either case; a breakdown flipping to truncated is the actionable signal to raise its max_groups setting.

panels.title: NAD Inventory Export Coverage
panels.description: Configured NADs selected for per-device series versus the exact ERS inventory total, as a trend. Selected converging on total is the healthy steady state; truncated=1 means inactive devices were omitted after active devices were prioritized -- raise the export selection ceiling if it stays truncated in production.

targets.expr: ise_nad_inventory_selected and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

ise_nad_inventory_total and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

ise_nad_inventory_truncated and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

panels.title: NAD Activity Group Coverage
panels.description: Volume-ranked NAD activity groups returned versus the exact pre-limit group count. In a healthy production environment returned and total trend together and truncated stays 0 -- this is now a paged scan sized for the full fleet, not a hard top-K sample. A sustained gap or truncated=1 is a real signal that the paged bound was exceeded, not expected-at-scale noise; raise dataconnect.max_groups if it persists. See the Refresh Surface panel below for the wider recency-ranked scan that feeds the full-inventory dead-switch accumulator.

targets.expr: ise_nad_activity_groups_returned and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

ise_nad_activity_groups_total and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

ise_nad_activity_groups_truncated and on() ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1))

panels.title: NAD Activity Refresh Surface Coverage (Recency-Ranked, Full Inventory)
panels.description: Recency-ranked NAD groups returned this cycle to refresh the high-water last-authentication accumulator, versus the exact pre-limit group count -- this is the surface that feeds the full-inventory dead-switch product (ise_nad_activity_last_authentication_timestamp), sized to cover the whole configured NAD fleet, not just the busiest devices. Returned and total should converge in a healthy production environment; truncated=1 is an actionable alert that the paged bound was exceeded -- raise the refresh ceiling rather than dismissing it as expected-at-scale.

targets.expr: ise_nad_activity_refresh_groups_returned and on() (((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)) and ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1)))

ise_nad_activity_refresh_groups_total and on() (((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)) and ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1)))

ise_nad_activity_refresh_truncated and on() (((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="dataconnect_nad_health",source="dataconnect"}) == 1)) and ((max(ise_dataset_up{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1) and (max(ise_dataset_fresh{dataset="devices",source="rest"}) == 1)))

panels.title: MnT Detail Coverage
panels.description: Successful MnT session-detail lookups ÷ selected active endpoints. Budget-bound: each 5-minute cycle fetches up to mnt_active_posture.max_requests_per_cycle (default 80) details, so under heavy session churn — or when active sessions exceed the 1000 selection ceiling — the ratio plateaus below 100%. Raise max_requests_per_cycle (max 250) and/or refresh_ttl to lift it.

panels.title: MnT Detail Truncated
panels.description: TRUNCATED means active-session candidates exceeded the configured selection ceiling (mnt_active_posture.max_sessions, capped at 1000), so posture detail was collected for only the first 1000 endpoints -- coverage and compliance reflect that subset. Shown as a trend so a shift from COMPLETE to TRUNCATED (rising active-session load) is visible before it happens again.

panels.title: Unknown Endpoint Profiles
panels.description: Data Connect reporting-snapshot endpoints whose profile is missing or unknown; some inventory attributes can synchronize with delay. Shown as a trend so growth is visible; some inventory attributes synchronize with delay so short blips are expected.

panels.title: Endpoints Stale 90d
panels.description: Endpoint reporting-snapshot rows not updated during the last 90 days; this is not exact real-time endpoint state. Shown as a trend so a growing 90-day-stale population is visible over time.

panels.title: Data Readiness (When Is Each Dataset Usable)
Dataset Readiness Right Now (Up + Fresh)
panels.description: 1 means the dataset has completed at least one successful collection within its freshness window and is safe to build a panel on -- true for snapshot datasets (most REST and Data Connect reporting views) as soon as it lights up. Two families need a caveat this boolean cannot express on its own: the four opt-in id-tail counters (dataconnect_accounting/posture/authentication/error_counters) reach this state after their cursor's very first cycle, but rate()/increase() queries over them need roughly one more scrape interval to leave that cold start behind -- watch the Id-Tail panels below for real tailing activity. endpoint_fleet reaches this state immediately but its *coverage* ramps over 24-48h -- watch the Endpoint Fleet Coverage Ramp panel below, not this boolean, for that dataset.

panels.title: Id-Tail Cursor Position (per view)
panels.description: High-water id committed by the incremental-tail engine for each opt-in counter view. A cursor that stops advancing while its owning dataset is enabled and up means the tail has stalled; compare against Events Folded per Cycle below.

panels.title: Id-Tail Events Folded per Cycle (per view)
panels.description: Rows folded into the incremental counters by the most recent tail cycle for each view. Zero is normal during a quiet period; a cursor that advances (previous panel) while this stays zero for a long stretch on a normally busy view is worth a second look. A dataset becomes meaningfully usable for rate()/increase() once this has been nonzero across at least one full scrape interval after the cursor's cold-start cycle -- there is no direct metric for elapsed time since cold start, so use the dataset's Last Success Age on the Collection Status table as a proxy.

panels.title: Id-Tail Cursor Resets (per view)
panels.description: Cumulative count of source-sequence resets (a backward id jump) for each incremental-tail view. Any increase restarts that view's ramp to usable -- rows since the reset are recounted from the new floor.

panels.title: Endpoint Fleet Coverage Ramp (24-48h, opt-in)
panels.description: Accumulated assessed endpoints divided by posture-applicable endpoints for the opt-in endpoint_fleet accumulator. This dataset is usable immediately for what it has accumulated so far, but full fleet coverage is expected to ramp toward ~100% over roughly 24-48h as the paged scan revisits the whole posture-applicable population -- do not expect or alert on 100% before that window has elapsed. Dark (no series) when endpoint_fleet.enabled is false.

panels.title: MnT Unpaged ActiveList Safety
panels.description: The cheap ActiveCount result, configured ceiling, and whether the exporter refused the unpaged ActiveList download.

panels.title: Exporter Build Identity
panels.description: Exact deployed source revision and supported Cisco ISE release. A revision of unknown means the packaging layer did not inject a build identity.
